title: 3 things that change when you chase God instead of a wife
caption:
3 things that change when you chase God instead of a wife.

Most men have the order backwards. They're hunting for the right woman — and can't figure out why it isn't working.

Here's what nobody tells them: the man worth marrying is built in the pursuit, not the finding.

Shift 1 — The hunger quiets. The desperate man leaks. He shape-shifts to be whatever she might want, texts first every time to quiet the ache, needs her approval to feel like a man. When God is the pursuit, that hunger gets fed somewhere real. You stop performing. You show up settled.

Shift 2 — Your days get ordered. Not as duty. As conversation. You open the Word because you need it. And slowly it organizes everything: your money, your mornings, who gets your hours. "Seek first his kingdom and his righteousness." — Matthew 6:33

Shift 3 — You become the man worth marrying. Not theoretically. Concretely. You keep your word. You have brothers who know your actual sin. You're the same guy when no one's watching. "He who finds a wife finds a good thing." — Proverbs 18:22. The verse describes the man he became first.

Stop hunting. Start becoming.

—

Comment "first" and I'll send you the link to ETHOS — the daily anchor for the man trying to walk with God.

theme: He chases God, not a wife.
